series_metadata.series_description: The Adjudication Committee Hearing Records are created for The Adjudicating Committee Hearings. These records contain the employer’s formal complaint “e.g. protest letters” the Bureau’s position on the issue, and/or the Employer Group’s formal complaint, statement of facts, complaint decision, compliant denial letter, and any cases, rules, or laws being relied upon by the parties. The decision Order drafted by the Committee Chairperson in Legal or the decision Order of the Administrator’s Designee when appealed to a second level hearing. These Orders may be appealed to Court by the Employer or the Employer Group. The Adjudicating Committee Hearing Records are scanned into the Universal Document Service (UDS). The Universal Document Service (UDS) is an imaging concept that stores any type of electronic file, such as Microsoft Office documents, Adobe Acrobat, audio/video, Computer Aided Design (CAD), etc. as well as scanned images.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Electronic: Adjudication Committee Hearing Records are drafted, kept, and published into a PDF in Legal Division’s Adjudicating SharePoint site. Scan final Portable Document Format (PDF) into Universal Document Service (UDS) Employer Services Policy Risk file and verify quality assurance. ten (10) years final adjudicating order, and ; Paper: ten (10) years final adjudicating order OR 30 days being converted an electronic record, and
